ogTitle: Willung South vs Wilsons Promontory: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Willung South and Wilsons Promontory.
heading: Willung South vs Wilsons Promontory — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Willung South, Victoria and Wilsons Promontory, Victoria. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Willung South has a lower overall climate risk score (39/100) compared to Wilsons Promontory (42/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Willung South or Wilsons Promontory safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Willung South has a flood risk score of 36/100, while Wilsons Promontory scores 50/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Willung South scores 49/100 for bushfire risk. Wilsons Promontory scores 48/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Willung South or Wilsons Promontory?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Willung South has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.75% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
